description:
Advances: specled.implementation_tier

Deliverable:
SpecLedEx.Realization.Implementation orchestrator; SpecLedEx.Realization.Closure.compute/2 walks MFA callee edges from the tracer side-manifest starting at declared implementation bindings, stopping at subject boundaries + out-of-project modules + already-visited MFAs; Closure.subject_for_mfa/2 with MFA ownership rule (realized_by.implementation wins; surface-file mapping with lexical tiebreak; otherwise shared helper); hash-ref composition (Cargo pattern) — closures reference other subjects' hashes by id + hash string, not canonical AST; shared-helper accounting inlines orphan MFAs into each caller's closure; Scenario 1 integration test (scenario_refactor_stable_test.exs).

Verification:
- Tests to pass:
  - mix test test/specled_ex/realization/closure_test.exs
  - mix test test/specled_ex/realization/implementation_test.exs
  - mix test test/integration/scenario_refactor_stable_test.exs --include integration
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - specled.implementation_tier.scenario.closure_walk_stops_at_boundary
  - specled.implementation_tier.scenario.shared_helper_inlined
  - specled.implementation_tier.scenario.ownership_lexical_tiebreak
  - specled.implementation_tier.scenario.refactor_does_not_drift (Scenario 1)

Allowed touches:
- lib/specled_ex/realization/implementation.ex
- lib/specled_ex/realization/closure.ex
- test/specled_ex/realization/implementation_test.exs
- test/specled_ex/realization/closure_test.exs
- test/integration/scenario_refactor_stable_test.exs

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/compiler/** (S3a owns tracer + xref)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/api_boundary.ex (S2)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/drift.ex (extended in S5b only)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/expanded_behavior.ex (S5a)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/use.ex (S5b)
- lib/specled_ex/coverage/**
- lib/specled_ex/coverage_triangulation.ex

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do NOT introduce a configurable closure_strategy (scope cut #1) — hardcode subject_boundary.
- Do NOT define Coverage.subject_for_mfa/1 here (scope audit #17); Closure owns ownership.
- Do NOT persist closure sets in state.json; recompute per-check.
- Hash-ref composition uses the string form "subject:<id>:hash:<hash>" in the hash input, not the referenced canonical AST.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] mix spec.check passes on specled_ex's own repo
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: UUID not available in this harness
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/<session-uuid>.jsonl

Commit: 4b43d32eb5090c32d6def8109267eb38a778c8fb
Files touched:
- lib/specled_ex/realization/closure.ex (new)
- lib/specled_ex/realization/implementation.ex (new)
- test/specled_ex/realization/closure_test.exs (new)
- test/specled_ex/realization/implementation_test.exs (new)
- test/integration/scenario_refactor_stable_test.exs (new)
- .spec/specs/implementation_tier.spec.md (stubs flipped to execute: true)
- .spec/state.json (index recomputed by mix spec.check)

Tests added:
- SpecLedEx.Realization.ClosureTest (8 tests, covers: closure_walks_tracer_edges, ownership_rule, shared_helper_accounting)
- SpecLedEx.Realization.ImplementationTest (9 tests, covers: hash_ref_composition, closure_walks_tracer_edges, dangling + drift)
- SpecLedEx.Integration.ScenarioRefactorStableTest (1 integration test, covers: scenario_refactor_stable — the gating criterion of Scenario 1)

Verification stubs flipped:
- mix test test/specled_ex/realization/closure_test.exs → execute: true
- mix test test/specled_ex/realization/implementation_test.exs → execute: true
- mix test test/integration/scenario_refactor_stable_test.exs --include integration → execute: true

Summary: Closure.compute/2 walks the tracer MFA edge manifest from a subject's declared implementation bindings, stopping at subject boundaries (ownership via realized_by.implementation first, then spec-meta.surface file-match with lexical tiebreak), out-of-project modules, and already-visited MFAs; orphans inline as shared helpers. Implementation.run/4 composes per-subject impl hashes with Cargo-style hash references ("subject:<id>:hash:<hex>") to other subjects — A's hash flips when B's impl hash flips, without inlining B's canonical AST. A deep_strip_meta pass inside the orchestrator (kept local to Implementation; Canonical module was not in Allowed touches) handles the line-meta on dot-expression form tuples that Canonical.strip_meta's shallow recursion misses — this is the mechanical enabler for the refactor_does_not_drift scenario.

Test results:
- mix test test/specled_ex/realization/closure_test.exs → 8 pass
- mix test test/specled_ex/realization/implementation_test.exs → 9 pass
- mix test test/integration/scenario_refactor_stable_test.exs --include integration → 1 pass
- Full suite: mix test --include integration → 313 tests, 0 failures
- mix spec.check → 20 pre-existing warnings against other still-draft subjects (coverage_capture, expanded_behavior_tier, triangulation, use_tier — S4/S5 work); ZERO findings against specled.implementation_tier. Prior ticket (q59.3) accepted the same pre-existing state.

title: S3b: implementation tier + subject-boundary closure
